biases.name: Fluency / Cognitive Ease
biases.definition: People prefer options that are easy to process and understand.

biases.name: Mere Exposure
biases.definition: Repeated exposure increases liking and recall.

biases.name: Anchoring
biases.definition: Initial high benchmark shapes value perception.

biases.name: Novelty / Distinctiveness
biases.definition: New or distinctive content captures attention.

biases.name: Authority
biases.definition: On-camera expertise and premium craft increase trust.

biases.name: Social Proof
biases.definition: People follow the crowd when uncertain.

biases.name: Loss Aversion
biases.definition: Fear of missing out motivates action.

biases.name: Choice Architecture
biases.definition: Designing options nudges decisions without restricting choice.

biases.name: Peak-End Rule
biases.definition: People remember the peak moment and the ending most.

biases.name: Framing
biases.definition: Presentation changes perception without changing facts.
